mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales signal robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and pushes S&P 500 forward returns upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS level against forward 20-day returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when RSAFS is above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a level consistent with 3% year-over-year inflation, markets anticipate tighter monetary policy that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: Positive forward 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 210, or the average return difference between triggered and non-triggered periods is less than 0.5% absolute.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment and decline in payroll employment constitutes a strong contractionary signal, as firms reduce headcount and job seekers increase, portending lower consumer spending and earnings.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by at least 0.2pp and PAYEMS decreases in the same month
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the joint signal fails to produce a negative mean return with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high interest rate environment (Fed funds above 4.5%), rising industrial production indicates potential overheating and margin compression from higher input costs, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The conditional correlation between INDPRO and forward 60-day returns is not significantly negative (z-score > -1.96) when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5, or the interaction term in a regression has p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below the 1300 level signal a weakening housing sector, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and depresses equity prices over short horizons.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in at least 45% of triggered instances, or the mean return difference between triggered and non-triggered periods is less than 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market with rising incomes, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, pushing equity prices higher.
failure_condition: The rank correlation of PAYEMS month-over-month change with forward 20-day returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or absolute returns in months with positive payrolls change are not significantly higher than in negative months.

mechanism_narrative: When both the federal funds rate and consumer price index are simultaneously elevated, it indicates that monetary policy is restrictive against a backdrop of high inflation, raising stagflation fears and depressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 35% of triggered instances, or the joint signal does not produce a significantly negative mean return (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a period of strong economic expansion (real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% YoY), a decline in industrial production acts as a leading indicator of impending recession, as it suggests weakening broader activity, thus lowering forward equity returns.
regime_hint: economic expansion above 2.5% YoY GDP growth
failure_condition: The negative correlation between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 60-day returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) when GDPC1 YoY growth exceeds 2.5%, or the regime-conditional signal replicates less than 60% of the time.
